content: Hey, I'm feeling a bit stuck in my career as a wedding planner. I've been running my own agency for years, but I'm not sure if I want to continue.

I understand how you feel. It can be challenging to maintain a career that requires so much attention to detail and pressure. What are some things you enjoy about your current career, and what are some things you're looking to change?

I love the opportunity to work with couples and help them plan their special day, but I'm feeling a bit burnt out from the constant stress and long hours. I've been thinking about exploring other options that would allow me to have more work-life balance.
It's great that you're recognizing the need for more balance in your life. Exploring other options is a great idea. Have you considered teaching or coaching to share your expertise with others?
Actually, I've been thinking about that. I've been taking some time to focus on my hobbies, like knitting and surfing. I've even started a knitting group in my local community in Los Angeles.
That sounds like a great way to unwind and stay creative. Knitting and surfing can be very fulfilling. How do you find time to balance your hobbies with your career and running your agency?

It can be challenging, but I prioritize my time. I make sure to fit in some knitting or surfing in the mornings, and I've been trying to stay organized by using a planner. I've also been enjoying my travel blogging hobby, and I might even start writing more about my experiences as a wedding planner.

That sounds like a great way to share your expertise and experiences with others. Have you considered incorporating your travel blogging into your career, maybe by creating a blog or YouTube channel focused on wedding planning and travel?

That's not a bad idea. I've actually been thinking about creating an online course teaching wedding planning skills. I've already started working on it, and I'm hoping to have it launched by the end of the summer, maybe around August 20th.
An online course sounds like a great project. What inspired you to start working on it, and how do you plan to balance the creation process with your other commitments?

I've always loved sharing my knowledge and expertise with others, and I think an online course will be a great way to do that. I've been working on it for a few months now, and I'm hoping to have a draft finished by the spring, maybe around April 1st. I've also been enjoying my work with a local wedding venue, and I might even include some information about their services in the course.
Using your work with the wedding venue in the course is a great idea. It will add a layer of depth to the course. Have you thought about how you'll promote your course once it's launched?

I've been thinking about that. I might reach out to some wedding planning organizations or bloggers to see if they'd be interested in partnering with me. I've also been considering doing some webinars or workshops to promote the course.

I think the most challenging part will be finding the time to do everything. I'm still running my agency and planning weddings, and I need to make sure I have enough time to focus on the course and promoting it. I've been trying to stay organized by using a calendar and setting reminders.

Time management can be difficult, especially when you have multiple projects and commitments. It's great that you're finding ways to stay organized and focused. Have you considered seeking out any additional support or resources to help you with the promotion process?

Actually, I've been thinking about hiring a virtual assistant to help me with the promotion. I've heard that they can be really helpful in getting the word out about a new course. I'm planning to reach out to some virtual assistants in the next few weeks, maybe around May 15th.
A virtual assistant can be a great resource for promoting your course. They can help you connect with the right people and get your course in front of the right audience.
